perturbation.original_text: Licensee may recoup the Guarantee Forfeiture Payment (as defined in Paragraph 3 of this Amendment below) from the sales of the IN HER SHOES Wireless Product.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee may recoup the Guarantee Forfeiture Payment (as defined in Paragraph 3 of this Amendment below) from the sales of any Wireless Product developed under this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change broadens the scope of recoupment. Initially, recoupment was limited to sales of the 'IN HER SHOES' Wireless Product. Now, Licensee can recoup from sales of any wireless product developed under the agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction because the Guarantee Forfeiture Payment is intended for specific products, expanding it to all products contradicts the purpose of recoupment being limited to specific products. This is contradiction in paragraph 2(b)(i)
perturbation.contradicted_text: (i) "IDIOCRACY" GUARANTEE AND ROYALTY: There shall be no Individual Property guarantee attributable to the targeted Release "IDIOCRACY". Licensee may recoup the guarantee Forfeiture Payment (as defined in Paragraph 3 of this Amendment below) from the sales of the IDIOCRACY Wireless Product.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited recoupment to the 'IN HER SHOES' Wireless Product. The changed text broadens this to 'any Wireless Product developed under this agreement.' The contradicted text specifies recoupment from sales of the 'IDIOCRACY Wireless Product'. By allowing recoupment from any wireless product, the changed text contradicts the original intent of the 'IDIOCRACY' guarantee, creating an inconsistency in how the Guarantee Forfeiture Payment is applied, as it expands the products from which recoupment can occur, which is an inconsistency as defined.

perturbation.original_text: In addition to any outstanding Guarantee payments which Licensee shall pay to Fox as set forth in Paragraph 3 of this Amendment below, Licensee shall pay to Fox a minimum recoupable guarantee of ***** dollars (US$*****) on or before ***** ("ICE AGE 2 Guarantee"), and ***** The omitted portions of this exhibit have been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to a request for confidential treatment under Rule 406 promulgated under the Securities Act of 1933. Source: GLU MOBILE INC, S-1/A, 3/19/2007 Fox shall earn, and Licensee shall pay the applicable Major Release Royalties as set forth in Paragraph 7(a)(i) of the Agreement in connection with the property "ICE AGE 2".
perturbation.changed_text: In addition to any outstanding Guarantee payments which Licensee shall pay to Fox as set forth in Paragraph 3 of this Amendment below, Licensee shall pay to Fox a minimum recoupable guarantee of ***** dollars (US$*****) on or before ***** ("ICE AGE 2 Guarantee"), and ***** The omitted portions of this exhibit have been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to a request for confidential treatment under Rule 406 promulgated under the Securities Act of 1933. Source: GLU MOBILE INC, S-1/A, 3/19/2007 Fox shall earn, and Licensee shall pay the applicable Major Release Royalties as set forth in Paragraph 7(a)(i) of the Agreement in connection with any other properties under the agreement".
perturbation.explanation: By changing "ICE AGE 2" to "any other properties under the agreement", the clause now mandates that royalties related to ICE AGE 2 must be paid to any other property. This directly contradicts the explicit wording in the original clause, and paragraph 2(c)(ii) that states that royalties collected from ICE AGE 2 products will be in connection with ICE AGE 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the avoidance of doubt, there shall be no cross- collateralization of the Royalties collected from Licensee's sale, license, distribution or other exploitation of the Wireless Products derived from the Property "ICE AGE 2", with the Royalties collected from any other Wireless Products released by Licensee under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies a clause that previously specified royalties from "ICE AGE 2" would be paid in connection with that property to "any other properties under the agreement". This directly contradicts the contradicted text which explicitly prohibits cross-collateralization of royalties between "ICE AGE 2" and any other wireless products. The change creates a situation where royalties from ICE AGE 2 could be used for other properties, violating the original agreement that those royalties are specific to ICE AGE 2.

perturbation.original_text: Consistent with paragraph 15(c) of the Agreement, Licensee shall forfeit the Guarantee paid to date of ***** dollars ***** The omitted portions of this exhibit have been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to a request for confidential treatment under Rule 406 promulgated under the Securities Act of 1933. Source: GLU MOBILE INC, S-1/A, 3/19/2007 (US$*****), and shall immediately pay the remaining Guarantee of ***** dollars (US$*****), equaling a total Guarantee payment by Licensee to Fox of ***** dollars (US$*****) ("Guarantee Forfeiture Payment").
perturbation.changed_text: Consistent with paragraph 15(c) of the Agreement, Licensee may opt to either forfeit the Guarantee paid to date of ***** dollars ***** The omitted portions of this exhibit have been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to a request for confidential treatment under Rule 406 promulgated under the Securities Act of 1933. Source: GLU MOBILE INC, S-1/A, 3/19/2007 (US$*****), or continue to make payments under the original agreement schedule, at Licensee's discretion. Choosing to not forfeit results in no Guarantee Forfeiture Payment being immediately due.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by giving the Licensee the option to not forfeit the guarantee, contradicting the original term requiring immediate forfeiture and the payment of the remaining guarantee. The addition of 'Licensee may opt to' contradicts the first part of the original clause ('Licensee shall forfeit'). This change directly contradicts the subsequent clauses that rely on the guarantee forfeiture payment, especially those in paragraphs 2(a)(ii) and 2(b)(i).
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Guarantee Forfeiture Payment may be recouped from Licensee's sales of the IN HER SHOES Wireless Product and the IDIOCRACY Wireless Product, as set forth in Paragraphs 2(a)(ii) and 2(b)(i) of this Amendment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction. The original text states that the Licensee "shall forfeit the Guarantee," implying a mandatory action. The changed text alters this to "Licensee may opt to either forfeit the Guarantee," giving the Licensee a choice. This directly contradicts the original requirement of mandatory forfeiture and conflicts with the contradicted text, which states, "The Guarantee Forfeiture Payment may be recouped," assuming that the Guarantee Forfeiture Payment is a certainty. The changed text makes this payment optional, hence introducing a direct conflict within the document.
